message:
fix(tools): prevent OOM on large reads, strict UTF-8 edits, grep column limit

Three safety improvements to built-in tools:

ReadTool — clamp line range to prevent huge allocations:
- When a file has millions of lines and no limit is specified, the
  formatter would allocate line-number-prefixed strings for all of them
  before truncation. Now clamps end_line to start_line + DEFAULT_MAX_LINES + 1
  so at most ~2001 lines are formatted, then truncate_head handles the rest.

EditTool — strict UTF-8 decoding:
- Replace String::from_utf8_lossy() with String::from_utf8() and return
  a clear error ("File contains invalid UTF-8 characters and cannot be
  safely edited as text") instead of silently replacing invalid bytes with
  U+FFFD, which corrupts binary files and causes phantom diffs.

GrepTool — add --max-columns=10000:
- Prevents ripgrep from reading entire minified JSON/JS lines (some >50MB)
  into memory, which could cause OOM. Lines longer than 10K chars are
  truncated in output, which is sufficient for match context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
